provider.name: Ministry of MSME (QCI as implementing partner)

benefits.name: Subsidy on cost of ZED certification
benefits.summary: Government bears a large share of the ZED certification fee (Bronze/Silver/Gold) for registered MSMEs, reducing the cost of quality-and-sustainability certification.
benefits.calculation_logic: Subsidy on certification cost: micro 80pct, small 60pct, medium 50pct. Additional 10pct for women/SC/ST-owned or NER/Himalayan/LWE/island/aspirational-district units, plus additional 5pct for SFURTI or MSE-CDP cluster members. Certification cost: Bronze 10000, Silver 40000, Gold 90000.

benefits.name: ZED pledge joining reward
benefits.summary: One-time limited-purpose reward of 10000 to each MSME on taking the ZED pledge, to be used within a defined period for a defined purpose.
benefits.calculation_logic: Flat 10000 per MSME on taking the ZED pledge; valid for a limited period after the pledge.

benefits.name: Handholding and zero-effect technology upgradation support
benefits.summary: Financial support of up to 5 lakh per ZED-certified MSME for consultancy handholding and for moving towards zero-effect solutions, pollution control or cleaner technology.
benefits.calculation_logic: Up to 5 lakh per MSME (up to 2 lakh for handholding/consultancy and up to 3 lakh for zero-effect technology), subsidized at micro 80pct, small 60pct, medium 50pct, with the same additional 10pct/5pct enhancements as certification.

benefits.name: Reimbursement for testing / system / product certification
benefits.summary: After achieving a ZED certification level, MSMEs can claim reimbursement on the cost of one testing, one management-system certification or one product certification.
benefits.calculation_logic: Subsidy on cost of one Testing OR one Management System OR one Product Certification after achieving a ZED level. Rupee cap to be confirmed from scheme guidelines (secondary sources cite up to 50000).

metadata.notes: Registration is free. Women-owned MSMEs are reported to get certification effectively free across levels. Max rupee value varies by level and category; compute from the percentage grid.

suggested_faqs.question: What is the main objective of MSME Sustainable (ZED) Certification Scheme?
suggested_faqs.answer: The main objective is to provide assistance and support under MSME Sustainable (ZED) Certification Scheme to eligible beneficiaries.

suggested_faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for MSME Sustainable (ZED) Certification Scheme?
suggested_faqs.answer: Businesses belonging to micro, small, medium, women_led, sc_st_entrepreneur in manufacturing, services may apply.
